reason: Response B shows greater emotional sophistication and nuance characteristic of a more capable model: it distinguishes intent from impact ("good intentions don't erase the sting"), explicitly avoids demanding forgiveness ("I'm not asking you to forget it"), and uses varied, textured sentence rhythm. Response A is competent but more formulaic and templated ("Your feelings are valid," "If there's anything I can do to make this right"), which reads like the smaller Haiku tier, leaving Sonnet as the author of B.
